chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No. D-346 of 2018 For hearing of CMA 1283/2023 Const. Petition No. D-464 of 2018 1.For orders on CMA 7256/2023 2.For orders on CMA 7254/2023 3.For orders on CMA 1082/2023 4.For hearing of CMA 1083/2023 Present:- Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro & Mr. Justice Arbab Ali Hakro Mr. Sikandar Ali Junejo, Advocate for applicants/petitioners in C.P.No.D-346 of 2018 a/w petitioner No.2. Mr. Khuda Bux Chohan, Advocate for respondents-SMC, Sukkur. Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Assistant A.G-Sindh. Date of Hearing & Order: 01-02-2024 O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J:- These petitions seeking directions to respondents/relevant Sukkur Municipal Corporation authorities to pay outstanding amount of overtime, part of salaries of the petitioners, were disposed of by a common order dated 22.09.2022, when learned counsel for respondents filed a breakup regarding outstanding amount of overtime, being part of petitioners’ salaries in view of Sindh People’s Municipality Sukkur Fire Brigade Staff (Overtime) Rules, 1976, and learned counsel for petitioners accepted calculation endorsed in the break up. And hence respondents were directed to pay the said amount to the petitioners. Thereafter, listed applications under Article 204 of the Constitution have been filed for taking action against the alleged contemnors for not complying with the Court’s orders. 2. Learned counsel for respondents has filed a computer generated table duly attested by Accounts Officer, SMC datelining in different columns, headed as paid month, year, cheque number, amount deposited in the bank along with details of bank accounts of employees, in which relevant amount was deposited.

Thereafter, listed applications under Article 204 of the Constitution have been filed for taking action against the alleged contemnors for not complying with the Court’s orders. 2. Learned counsel for respondents has filed a computer generated table duly attested by Accounts Officer, SMC datelining in different columns, headed as paid month, year, cheque number, amount deposited in the bank along with details of bank accounts of employees, in which relevant amount was deposited. Learned counsel for petitioners and the petitioner present in person have, however, disputed Page 2 of 2 even the figures in the table and amount deposited in their bank accounts. Since now the dispute between the parties appears to be on factual aspects of the matter, listed applications are disposed of and the petitioners are directed to approach the Appellate Tribunal Local Councils Sindh at Sukkur, notified on 10.11.2023 for redressal of their grievances. Office to place a signed copy of this order in captioned connected matter. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
